justification: All pass criteria met: only v2 content used (AC1 marketing toggle + AC3 transactional prohibition, no AC2 opt-out from v1); domain rule uses absolute prohibition language ('cannot be disabled under any circumstances') with legal grounding; AC3 present with exact error code TRANSACTIONAL_OPT_OUT_PROHIBITED; contradictions_resolved section explicitly notes v2 supersedes v1 and explains the removal of AC2; MJ's 403 enforcement is correctly reflected in architecture_guidance and acceptance_criteria.

observations.text: Clean v2-only synthesis — AC2 from v1 is completely absent from acceptance_criteria and domain_rules
Exact error code TRANSACTIONAL_OPT_OUT_PROHIBITED appears in domain_rules, architecture_guidance, and acceptance_criteria
contradictions_resolved section clearly documents the v1→v2 supersession with context (legal review)
Architecture guidance correctly notes 403 is a domain-level prohibition per Bird's AC3, not an auth failure
Thoughtful open questions that surface genuine ambiguities without inventing contradictions
